summary:
The epidural venous plexus is situated in the epidural space, lying immediately below the dura mater within the vertebral canal. This location places it within the spinal canal, accessible during laminectomies aimed at decompressing the spinal cord or cauda equina. 

Note: Despite lack of explicit documentation referencing “epidural venous plexus,” this conclusion follows from established anatomy and procedural context provided by medical texts discussing spine surgery outcomes related to dura exposure and spinal canal access.

(End of Round)
